target.name: Club for Growth Action
target.notes_excerpt: Conservative super PAC affiliated with Club for Growth. Supports limited-government, free-market candidates. Major donors include Peter Thiel and Richard Uihlein. Focuses on tax cuts, deregulation, free trade, school choice. No specific AI policy position — general deregulatory stance would extend to opposing AI regulation. Founded 2010 as independent expenditure arm.

source.name: Dustin Moskovitz
source.notes_excerpt: Dustin Moskovitz is a co-founder of Facebook and Asana, with an estimated net worth of $17.4 billion as of May 2025 according to Forbes. He co-founded the philanthropic organization Good Ventures with his wife Cari Tuna in 2011. Moskovitz and Tuna are major funders of AI safety research, having donated $1 million to Future of Life Institute and $30 million to OpenAI's non-profit in 2017. Open Phil

target.name: Helion Energy
target.notes_excerpt: Private nuclear fusion company building the Orion plant in Chelan County, WA, targeting power delivery to Microsoft data centers by 2028 under the world's first fusion power purchase agreement. Backed by Sam Altman ($350M+) and SoftBank; raised $425M Series F in Jan 2025 at a $5.4B valuation. Directly tied to AI compute energy demands.

target.name: Universidad de Buenos Aires
target.notes_excerpt: Argentina's largest public university, accounts for ~40% of national research output. Has AI-focused labs: IALAB (Law Faculty — AI governance, judicial AI guidelines) and LIAA (Applied AI Lab in Computer Science). IALAB published guide on responsible use of generative AI for judiciary, modeled on MIT Task Force approach. Key institution in Latin American AI policy discourse.

evidence: U.S. Senator Mike Rounds (R-S.D.), co-chair of the Senate AI Caucus

source.name: Mike Rounds
source.notes_excerpt: Mike Rounds is a Republican U.S. Senator from South Dakota who was sworn into the United States Senate on January 6th, 2015. He serves as co-chair of the Senate AI Caucus and serves on five committees: Senate Appropriations; Armed Services; Banking, Housing and Urban Affairs; Indian Affairs; and the Select Committee on Intelligence. He previously served as the 31st governor of South Dakota from 20

target.name: Senate AI Caucus
target.notes_excerpt: Bipartisan U.S. Senate caucus on artificial intelligence, launched March 2019 by Sens. Martin Heinrich (D-NM) and Rob Portman (R-OH). Original members included Schatz (D-HI), Gardner (R-CO), Peters (D-MI), Ernst (R-IA). After Portman's retirement (2023), Mike Rounds (R-SD) became co-chair. Heinrich named one of TIME's 100 Most Influential People in AI. The caucus works toward responsible AI policy
